modules.title: Browser-side Kernel
modules.summary: Session, run queue, loop, compaction, and the future browser-side brain mainline.

modules.title: Observability And Audit
modules.summary: Cutover-critical diagnostics, audit tail, runtime history, replay, and observability export read surfaces are landed; broader export and event-source breadth stays deferred.
shipped_scope: runtime.capture_diagnostics action (kernel/bridge/runner/site/debug snapshot)
runtime.history resource (loop telemetry entries)
audit.tail resource (config/skill/host/loop lifecycle, 500 entries, 7d retention)
audit.intervention resource (intervention lifecycle tracking)
observability.replay resource (loop/audit/intervention/continuity replay)
observability export contracts/builders for timeline/summary/rawEventTail (contracts/core/site-runtime, test-backed)
shared MV3 resource.read projection for observability.timeline/summary/rawEventTail
kernel diagnostics facade (kernel.captureDiagnostics)
error lifecycle tracking (active/cleared/recent/empty states)
provider routing diagnostics (LLM profile resolution status)
deferred_scope: shared debug dump/export action (operator-level bulk export)
richer future event-source breadth from full skill SDK/studio and wider browser automation composites
modules.deferral_rationale: Gate F (Operability) is satisfied. ISSUE-143 landed the export schema/builder, ISSUE-161 closed the page-action handoff boundary that previously blocked browser-automation event production, and ISSUE-166 exposed observability.timeline/summary/rawEventTail through the shared MV3 read path. Remaining work is intentionally post-cutover breadth: bulk dump/export and richer future event sources.

modules.title: Intervention And Handoff
modules.summary: Human intervention, runtime handoff, escalation, shared audit/read surfaces, and operator resolution flow around automation and runtime failures.
shipped_scope: runtime handoff contract for verify_failed/runtime_blocked automation failures
kernel and MV3 intervention request/resolve/cancel/timeout lifecycle with durable restart round-trip
runtime.summary.interventions and audit.intervention shared read surfaces
cross-tab/window coordination plus structured stale/timeout escalation audit entries
shared sidepanel resolve/reject UI for pending intervention requests
deferred_scope: richer studio/operator workflow polish beyond the shared sidepanel management surface
modules.deferral_rationale: Current cutover-critical intervention scope is landed. Remaining UI polish belongs to adjacent AI-surface/studio work and no longer justifies keeping intervention-handoff itself in partial status.

modules.title: AI Surface Control Plane
modules.summary: Cutover-critical product control-plane surfaces across runtime, config, skills, hosts, and northbound action/resource boundaries are landed; fuller studio and lifecycle breadth stays deferred.
shipped_scope: descriptor-owned northbound action projection controls (audiences/defaultExposed/confirmPolicy/executionTarget) across tool/chat/MCP projections
shared runtime/config/skills/hosts bootstrap and summary resource surfaces through readAiSurfaceResource() / MV3 resource.read
shared control-plane actions for runtime.capture_diagnostics/runtime.clear_error/config.update/skills.install|enable|disable|uninstall/hosts.connect|disconnect|set_default
provider routing northbound surface through config.summary/config.update model + model.routing state
shared sidepanel management consumer that reads and mutates only the shared AI-surface contract
deferred_scope: full Skill Studio/lifecycle/versioning UI beyond the shared management consumer
broader metadata-registry and audience-projection hardening across every future consumer
bridge-side MCP server/transport beyond the current descriptor-derived handoff contract
modules.deferral_rationale: Gate G's minimum self-awareness surface is satisfied. ISSUE-153 and ISSUE-156 locked descriptor-owned action projection semantics, ISSUE-159/163/164 wired provider routing through the shared control-plane contract and runtime-owned rehydrate path, and ISSUE-093 closed the shared sidepanel management consumer. Remaining work is intentionally post-cutover product and export breadth.

modules.title: Site Runtime And Browser Automation
modules.summary: Current cutover-critical site runtime and browser automation scope is landed; wider automation breadth and export surfaces remain deferred.
shipped_scope: active-tab-only site runtime boundary, explicit invoke-only injection, and real page-hook injection chain
page.query/click/fill/press_key/screenshot plus tabs.list/get_active/navigate runtime paths through MV3/runtime-services
site.fetch_with_session active-tab session bridge
background lane baseline with explicit target routing, DOM stabilization policy, and non-active-tab page action coverage
cutover docs that lock Tier 1/Tier 2/Tier 3 browser automation scope
deferred_scope: Tier 2/Tier 3 breadth such as page.scroll/select_option/hover, tabs.create/close, and stealth/computer mode
download/export composites such as screenshot_with_highlight, download_image, and download_chat_images
future provider-bridge unification if page/tabs/site runtime is later routed through FamilyProvider by default
modules.deferral_rationale: Soft Gate 2's cutover-critical scope is landed and no longer needs an executable live slice. Remaining work is intentionally outside the current cutover bar or belongs to later product/export breadth, so the module should be tracked as shipped-with-deferred-scope rather than partial.

modules.title: Execution Host And Bridge
modules.summary: Cutover-critical JS runner, offscreen bridge, MV3 runtime wiring, browser-only local file host, and exec-capable remote host routing are landed; broader remote-host product breadth stays deferred.
shipped_scope: JS runner timeout/cancel/health/isolation with MV3 offscreen bridge wiring
browser-only local execution host adapter for host.read/write/edit and offscreen diagnostics
exec-capable remote host path with transport config, health/degraded handling, and multi-remote discovery/rehydrate baseline
capability-aware hosts.summary/get/list surfaces that distinguish file-only vs exec-capable hosts
default host and host.exec routing that prefer exec-capable hosts instead of treating local as a universal default
deferred_scope: broader operator UX and fleet-management polish beyond the current host summaries/actions
additional remote transport/providers beyond the current cutover path
old host_bash-style homogeneous host parity where local and remote hosts pretend to be interchangeable
modules.deferral_rationale: Gate C is satisfied. ISSUE-038/073/125/126/127/134/157/165 closed the cutover-critical host path, including remote exec, health, transport config, multi-remote rehydrate, and capability-aware default routing. Remaining work is broader host productization, not an executable cutover gap.

modules.title: Provider And Profile Routing
modules.summary: Cutover-critical provider registry, profile routing, escalation policy, and shared control-plane routing baseline are landed; broader non-kernel rollout and provider-policy hardening stay deferred.
shipped_scope: provider registry, health negotiation, ordered profile chain, and retry escalation in packages/kernel
lane-aware routing plus runtime-owned baseline capability requirements for primary/compaction/title paths
shared control-plane model + model.routing contract for default/fallback/lane override state
runtime-services rehydrate of shared model.routing state back into runtime-owned provider profile config
provider routing diagnostics surfaced through shared runtime/diagnostics paths
deferred_scope: non-kernel call-site rollout to every future provider consumer
broader provider-policy hardening beyond the current runtime-owned baseline
richer operator tooling around provider ownership and policy governance
modules.deferral_rationale: The provider/profile routing mainline no longer lacks an executable live slice. ISSUE-150 resolved kernel-local taxonomy/reusable route resolution, ISSUE-159 exposed the shared control-plane update surface, and ISSUE-163/164 completed routing override management plus runtime-owned rehydrate. Remaining work is broader rollout and hardening, not a current mainline coverage gap.

modules.title: Old Product Replacement Loop
modules.summary: Cutover-critical proof that the rewritten browser extension can replace one old Plugin/Skill capability chain end-to-end is shipped; broader Skill Studio, versioning, and legacy ecosystem breadth stays deferred.
modules.milestone_rationale: The previous issue workflow closed many small review findings while leaving the larger old-product replacement claim unproven. This module grouped work around one verifiable product loop such as install -> persist -> enable -> invoke -> observe.
shipped_scope: ISSUE-172 proved install -> persist/restart -> enable -> skills.invoke -> tabs.get_active -> audit.tail through the shared MV3 runtime
ISSUE-173 proved skills.install setupPlan materializes package files under canonical mem://skills/<skillId>/... storage and survives restart
ISSUE-174 proved installed skill.json manifests register persisted package handlers and invoke them through the JS runner
ISSUE-175 proved package actions, site metadata, permissions, tags, and active-tab requirements are discoverable through shared skills.summary/runtime.bootstrap
ISSUE-176 proved the sidepanel Skills catalog consumes the same shared skills.summary.items and operates package-backed skills through existing lifecycle actions
ISSUE-177 consolidated the gate proof and separated shipped cutover-critical proof from deferred product breadth
ISSUE-178 exposed package lifecycle/version readiness through shared skills.summary and sidepanel catalog
ISSUE-179 connected shared skills.rollback to BrowserVFS rehydrate, package registry refresh, summary/invoke readback, and audit.tail evidence

ISSUE-180 proved shared Studio/package authoring data can create/update package-backed skills, auto-snapshot prior package state, refresh registry, invoke updated code, rollback, and read audit evidence

ISSUE-181 proved enabled package-backed skills can declare runtime event subscriptions and receive runtime.route.after through runtime.event.dispatch using the existing skills.invoke, JS Runner, and audit.tail path

ISSUE-184 proved the release-facing path in real Chromium MV3: package handlers execute in a sandbox runner under CSP and can call back into shared capabilities such as memfs.read with audit.tail evidence
ISSUE-185 made the Level 2 release acceptance evidence refresh one-command reproducible through bun run release:acceptance
deferred_scope: interactive version selection UI
diff/preview for package updates
rollback confirmation and richer Studio UX around the shared rollback action
bulk migration of every old plugin ecosystem affordance
post-cutover product polish around package browsing, install sources, and studio workflows
modules.deferral_rationale: ISSUE-172 through ISSUE-181 prove one representative old product capability loop end-to-end on the shared vNext runtime and product surface, including authoring, install, update, package persistence, discovery, sidepanel management, invocation, rollback, event subscription, and audit evidence. ISSUE-184 adds real Chromium MV3 CSP proof, and ISSUE-185 provides the one-command release acceptance evidence refresh. Remaining items are broader product/ecosystem breadth and no longer block the cutover-critical replacement proof.

modules.title: Skill Studio Completion Loop
modules.summary: Product-level Skill Studio, lifecycle, versioning, rollback readiness, and author/update surfaces now use the shared AI-surface path; richer editor and migration breadth stays deferred.
modules.cutover_gate: Full product completion after Level 2 proof
modules.milestone_rationale: ISSUE-172 through ISSUE-177 proved the cutover-critical executable Skill replacement chain. Full browser plugin refactor completion now needs the product-level Skill Studio/versioning lane to consume the existing lifecycle/version engine through shared AI-surface resources instead of remaining hidden as deferred breadth.
shipped_scope: shared skills.summary version/rollback readiness surface for package-backed skills
sidepanel Skills catalog consumption of the shared version/rollback surface
shared skills.rollback action that restores latest trusted package snapshots through BrowserVFS and records rollback evidence
sidepanel package editor that builds setupPlan from manifest JSON, handler source, and SKILL.md instead of using a private Studio registry
shared skills.install create/update path that writes mem://skills/<skillId> package files and refreshes package registry immediately
automatic snapshot of previous package state before update so versionSurface, skills.rollback, skills.invoke, and audit.tail prove rollback
deferred_scope: interactive version selection and rollback confirmation UI
package diff/preview before update
bulk migration of legacy plugin ecosystem affordances
richer editor polish and package source browsing
modules.deferral_rationale: ISSUE-178 through ISSUE-180 close the product-level lifecycle/version/author-update chain through shared AI-surface resources and actions. Remaining items are workflow breadth and UX polish, not blockers for the browser plugin refactor's representative Skill Studio completion loop.

modules.title: BrowserVFS
modules.summary: mem:// semantics, IndexedDB persistence, package discovery, retention, and VFS error-path correctness.

modules.title: Skill Runtime, SDK, And Studio
modules.summary: Executable skill packaging, lifecycle, SDK authoring, and future studio surfaces.

modules.title: Repo Workflow And DX
modules.summary: Workflow guardrails, documentation hygiene, CI, lint, and backlog planning ergonomics for parallel agents.
